request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We must return a JSON object with a "verdicts" array, one per candidate_id. Each verdict has "candidate_id", "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge" (true/false), "reason" (short reason), and "category_if_not" (allowed values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other).

We need to accept an item only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence.

Looking at the candidates, all seem to be operational sequences, parameter specs, fault diagnostic rules, or application guidance directly from the guideline, with specific section references, evidence quotes, and page numbers. They appear to be specific, actionable knowledge items. There is no front matter, TOC, generic definitions, duplicates (all unique), or unsupported summaries. They all match the guideline content. 
However, I'll quickly check each one:

cand_51723ac8d8447d16: "Generic Zone Setpoint Separation" - 5.3.2.2, says each zone shall have separate occupied and unoccupied heating and cooling setpoints. This is operational knowledge, a parameter specification. Valid.

cand_359f1c93da5b9499: Active Setpoint Selection Based on Zone Group Mode - 5.3.2.3, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_ea4f8c57d9c6270a: Setpoint Overlap and Limit Interlocks - 5.3.2.4, operational sequence (prevention of invalid setpoint relationships). Valid.

cand_329e90a6a842ef85: Default Local Setpoint Adjustment Limits - 5.3.2.5.b, parameter spec. Valid.

cand_3d7bd7db4940752c: Single Setpoint Adjustment Thermostat Behavior - 5.3.2.5.c, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_10c19e0cfd6216ee: Local Setpoint Adjustment Mode Restriction - 5.3.2.5.d, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_3e7c14efd166ac32: Demand Limiting Freezes Local Setpoint Adjustment - 5.3.2.5.e, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_5d28b9c9272a9302: Window Open Setpoint Override - 5.3.2.8, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_2a97e49855de1763: Window Open During Non-Occupied Mode Alarm - 5.3.2.8, fault diagnostic rule. Valid.

cand_fdc9b2c6ab43a200: Occupancy-Based Setpoint Setback - 5.3.2.9.a, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_099596520c408b93: Occupancy Sensor Setpoint Restoration - 5.3.2.9.b, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_bfb3715bac7dc0e4: Thermostat Override Button Calls Occupied Mode - 5.3.3, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_4cf6d413cc69ee96: Heating Loop Enable/Disable - 5.3.4.1.a, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_4234a744e39441e7: Cooling Loop Enable/Disable - 5.3.4.1.b, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_579c87decfe7452d: Zone Loop Control Type Requirement - 5.3.4.4, operational sequence (specification of control logic). Valid.

cand_34db58a9328b9439: Zone State Determination - 5.3.5, operational sequence. Valid.

cand_472c4d28f04a9b7f: High Temperature Level 4 Alarm - 5.3.6.1.a.1, fault diagnostic rule. Valid.

cand_c09b864c97fd781a: High Temperature Level 3 Alarm - 5.3.6.1.a.2, fault diagnostic rule. Valid.

cand_46a0dd7e2d55d208: Low Temperature Level 4 Alarm - 5.3.6.1.b.1, fault diagnostic rule. Valid.

cand_0235168826413680: Low Temperature Level 3 Alarm - 5.3.6.1.b.2, fault diagnostic rule. Valid.

cand_2f7e937c26fce12c: Consider Shorter Zone Temperature Alarm Delay for Critical Zones - 5.3.6.1, application guidance. Valid.

All are valid. So for each, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge: true, reason can be something like "Specific operational sequence from Section X", and category_if_not null.

But we must output strictly the requested JSON format.
